DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF JOSEPH LUEBBERKE'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. COURT FINDS THAT INSUFFICIENT NOTICE WAS PROVIDED PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 1005 BASED UPON THE FILING DATE OF DECEMBER 30, 2013. HOWEVER, COURT ALSO NOTES THAT, BASED ON ITS UNDERSTANDING OF THE FACTS, THE LEGAL AUTHORITY FOR PLAINTIFF'S MOTION ARISES UNDER CCP SECTION 2032.610, NOT THE GENERAL PROVISION OF SECTION 2031.210. SEE ALSO WEIL AND BROWN, ET AL., CAL. PRAC. GUIDE: CIV. PRO BEFORE TRIAL (THE RUTTER GROUP 2013) 8:1602 ET SEQ. THE PARTIES ARE ADVISED TO MEET AND CONFER CONCERNING THE LEGAL AUTHORITIES AND MATERIALS DISCOVERABLE BY PLAINTIFF FROM ALL MEDICAL EXAMINATIONS PRIOR TO RE-FILING ANY MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO-TEM: NILS ROSENQUEST; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Pltff'S Medical Examination
Off Calendar Feb-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Medical Examination is off calendar per moving party. Judge Pro Tem: Robert Kane; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Pltff'S Medical Examination
Off Calendar Jan-07-2014 Continued To Feb-03-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

Mtn To Compel Production Of Docs; Notice
Off Calendar Jan-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David W. Ammons, Deputy City Attorney, 415.554.3954, appearing for the Defendant City and County of San Francisco;
No appearance by any party or counsel.

No argument held. The Court adopts the tenative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF JOSEPH LUEBBERKE'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Motion is denied without prejudice. Court finds that insufficient notice was provided pursuant to C.C.P. Section 1005 based upon the filing date of December 30, 2013. However, Court also notes that, based on its understanding of the facts, the legal authority for plaintiff's motion arises under CCP Section 2032.610, not the general provision of section 2031.210. See also Weil and Brown, et al., CAL. PRAC. GUIDE: CIV. PRO BEFORE TRIAL (The Rutter Group 2013) 8:1602 et seq. The parties are advised to meet and confer concerning the legal authorities and materials discoverable by plaintiff from all medical examinations prior to re-filing any motion to compel production. Order signed. Judge Pro-Tem: Nils Rosenquest; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. =(302/JPT)

(Amended) Notice Of Renewed Motion And Moton For Independent Psychological Examination

Off Calendar Aug-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
